# Let me check all transitions to understand the movement pattern
print("All transitions:")
for i, t in enumerate(transitions):
    print(f"  Step {i}: {t.action}")
    if t.before_frame and t.after_frame:
        before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in t.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']}
        after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in t.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']}
        # Find green block
        for n in t.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
            if n['color'] == 'N':
                print(f"    Green block at {n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check current green block position
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"\nCurrent green: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The green block moves 2 cells at a time in a 3x3 grid within the frame
# Current position: row 38, col 10 (top-middle)
# Possible positions: rows 38, 40, 42; cols 8, 10, 12

for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'g' and n['pixels'] == 32:
        print(f"Gray frame: boundary={n['boundary']}")
        print(f"  Children: {n.get('children', [])}")

# Let me also check the adjacency to understand the frame structure
adj = current_frame.segmentation['adjacency_list']
for pair in adj:
    i, j = pair
    ni = current_frame.segmentation['nodes'][i]
    nj = current_frame.segmentation['nodes'][j]
    if ni['color'] == 'N' or nj['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green adjacent to: {ni['color'] if ni['color']=='N' else nj['color']} (id={i if ni['color']=='N' else j})")

# Try moving DOWN to return to original position, then explore
action([('DOWN')])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
